---
title: "*Using DWSIM to Create Binary Phase Diagrams* Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---

{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}

# Intro to *Using DWSIM to Create Binary Phase Diagrams*

Binary phase diagrams are commonly used to visualize vapor–liquid equilibrium behavior and to understand how mixtures separate during processes such as distillation. While these diagrams can be constructed analytically for simple systems, simulation tools allow engineers to generate them quickly for more complex mixtures.

This video demonstrates how to use **DWSIM**, an open-source process simulator, to generate binary phase diagrams and explore vapor–liquid equilibrium behavior.

---

## Using DWSIM to Create Binary Phase Diagrams

This video shows how binary phase diagrams can be generated using the **DWSIM process simulation software**, including how to specify components, choose thermodynamic models, and plot equilibrium data.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/5NUchIg8q7E?si=VRyVOkM4ANTQn37s" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

DWSIM  
: An open-source [process simulation software package](https://dwsim.org/) used to model chemical processes, perform thermodynamic calculations, and simulate unit operations such as reactors, distillation columns, and heat exchangers.

---

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### Two videos back

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 2**  
*Distillation Examples: Binary Phase Diagram, Raoult's law*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/wc6gv7lNtI0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/86-EquilibriumSeparationStages2Visuals.qmd)

### Previous video

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 3**  
*Liquid-Liquid Extraction Example*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/jcChVoELvFo), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/86.5-EquilibriumSeparationStages3Visuals.qmd)

### Next video

**Just Try to Break This Law! I Dare You!**  
*Intro to 1st Law*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/ZHWT9Jw7fzY), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/Intro1stLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/89-Intro1stLawVisuals.qmd)

### Two videos forward

**Add Friction and Work to Bernoulli? Here’s What Really Happens!**  
*The Mechanical Energy Balance and the Moody Chart*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/Da48oZb9oNw), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/MechanicalEnergyBalanceVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/90-MechEngBalVisuals.qmd)
